title: Korean Oi Muchim (Spicy Cucumber Salad)

subs.name: thinly sliced radish (mooli) or blanched bean sprouts — both absorb the dressing equally well
1 tsp red chilli powder + 1 tsp soy sauce mixed into a paste for a quicker version
fresh lemon juice — slightly different brightness but equally refreshing

steps.text: Slice the cucumbers into rounds about 4–5 mm thick or cut on a diagonal into ovals. Transfer to a colander, toss with 1 tsp salt and set aside for 10 minutes to sweat out excess water — this keeps the salad crisp rather than diluted.
Rinse the cucumber briefly under cold water to remove excess salt, then squeeze in small handfuls to press out remaining moisture. Pat dry on a clean kitchen cloth.
Combine gochujang, minced garlic, vinegar, sugar and gingelly oil in a bowl and stir into a smooth dressing.
Add the cucumber and sliced spring onion to the dressing. Toss well until every piece is coated. Taste and adjust — add more vinegar for brightness or a pinch more sugar to temper the heat.
Scatter sesame seeds on top and serve immediately as a side dish, or refrigerate for up to 4 hours. The salad is best while the cucumber is still pleasantly crisp.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cucumber → thinly sliced radish (mooli) or blanched bean sprouts — both absorb the dressing equally well; gochujang → 1 tsp red chilli powder + 1 tsp soy sauce mixed into a paste for a quicker version; vinegar → fresh lemon juice — slightly different brightness but equally refreshing
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Oi muchim requires no cooking and suits an Indian kitchen perfectly — all ingredients are available in any Indian market. Regular Indian cucumbers (vellarikkai in Tamil, kheera in Hindi) work well; salt them for a full 15 minutes if the skin is thick. Gochujang can be substituted with red chilli paste or a mix of Kashmiri chilli powder and a drop of soy sauce. This salad pairs beautifully with any Indian rice meal as a tangy, crunchy side.
